varieties.name: Mammoth Navy
varieties.origin: Early Multiflora Gigantea type, developed early 20th century
varieties.traits: Deep purple-blue flowers, large ruffled blooms, 250cm vine height
varieties.growing_notes: Requires only 10-11 hours daylight to flower - IDEAL for Brisbane subtropical conditions. Sow April-May for best results. Heat tolerant. Resists powdery mildew better than Spencer types.
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection (Queensland), Eden Seeds (Lower Beechmont, QLD), Happy Valley Seeds

varieties.name: Mammoth White
varieties.origin: Early Multiflora Gigantea type, developed early 20th century
varieties.traits: Pure white flowers, large size, 250cm vine height
varieties.growing_notes: Early flowering (10-11 hours daylight) - PERFECT for Brisbane heat. Plant April-May. Tolerates humidity better than late-flowering types.
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection (Queensland), Mr Fothergill's Australia

varieties.name: Mammoth Mix
varieties.traits: Wide color range: salmon, pink, rose, blue, lilac, white
varieties.growing_notes: Flowers early and continues into summer with regular cutting. Suitable for Brisbane subtropical climate. Sow April-May.
varieties.availability: Happy Valley Seeds, Mr Fothergill's Australia

varieties.name: Spring Sunshine Champagne
varieties.traits: Pale creamy blush, elegant color, long stems
varieties.growing_notes: Flowers with 12 hours daylight (mid-season). Good choice for Brisbane if sown in April. More heat tolerant than Spencer types.

varieties.name: Knee-Hi Mix
varieties.traits: Semi-dwarf 60-75cm height, bushy growth, mixed colors (violet, crimson, lilac, purple, white)
varieties.growing_notes: IDEAL for Brisbane containers and small gardens. Requires minimal support. Heat resistant. Dwarf habit reduces mildew issues. Perfect for patios.
varieties.availability: Hume Seeds, The Seed Collection, San Diego Seed Company

varieties.name: Cupid Series
varieties.origin: Developed 1890s, dwarf mutation of original varieties
varieties.traits: Very compact 20-25cm height, trailing/cascading habit, rose-pink and white bicolor
varieties.growing_notes: PERFECT for Brisbane hanging baskets and window boxes. No support needed. Cascades beautifully. More heat tolerant than climbing types. Suitable for subtropical balcony gardens.

varieties.name: Pink Cupid
varieties.traits: Dwarf 20-30cm, bicolored pink and white blooms
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane containers. No staking required. Heat tolerant for dwarf type.

varieties.name: Cupani
varieties.origin: Sicily 1699, original wild type sent by Father Francisco Cupani
varieties.traits: Deep purple standards, maroon-red wings, bicolor, 180-250cm vine
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, shorter stems than modern types
varieties.growing_notes: HEAT TOLERANT - more suitable for Brisbane than modern types. Early blooming. Incredible fragrance compensates for smaller size. Historical significance. More disease resistant.
varieties.availability: Diggers Club, Select Seeds, widespread availability Australia

varieties.name: Matucana
varieties.origin: Pre-1700s, very similar to original Cupani, may be 20th century Peruvian discovery
varieties.traits: Magenta-purple standards, deep purple wings, bicolor, 200-250cm
varieties.growing_notes: HEAT TOLERANT - suitable for Brisbane subtropical climate. Said to be most fragrant sweet pea. Early blooming. More humidity resistant than Spencer types.
varieties.availability: Diggers Club, The Seed Collection, widely available Australia

varieties.name: Painted Lady
varieties.origin: England 1730s-1737, sport from Cupani, first named cultivar
varieties.traits: Rose-pink standards, pale pink wings, bicolor, 200-250cm

varieties.name: America
varieties.traits: Bright red with creamy white streaks, striped pattern, 90cm vine

varieties.name: Windsor
varieties.traits: Deep purple blooms, large ruffled flowers, 180-240cm
varieties.growing_notes: Requires cool conditions. In Brisbane, sow May for best chance. Provide afternoon shade. Watch for mildew. One of more reliable Spencer types for marginal climates.
varieties.availability: Frohloff's Sweet Peas (Queensland), Australia's Sweet Pea Specialists, Sweet Pea Gardens

varieties.name: Nimbus
varieties.traits: Cream/grayish-white with dramatic dark blue-violet streaks, 180-240cm

varieties.name: April in Paris
varieties.traits: Cream with purple eye-liner and picotée edge, 180-200cm
varieties.growing_notes: WEATHER TOLERANT - bred for heat and humidity resistance. Excellent choice for Brisbane. Combines fragrance with modern size. Better mildew resistance than Spencer types.
varieties.availability: Renee's Garden (USA), Select Seeds, specialty suppliers

varieties.name: Erewhon
varieties.origin: Dr. Keith Hammett, New Zealand - reverse bicolor breakthrough
varieties.traits: Blue-pink bicolor, reverse coloring (lighter standards, darker wings), 180-200cm
varieties.growing_notes: HEAT TOLERANT modern grandiflora. Suitable for Brisbane with April-May sowing. World's first reverse bicolor. Hammett breeding provides better weather resistance.

varieties.name: Cupani
varieties.growing_notes: Small blooms, intense fragrance. Only attempt in hill stations during December-February. Early flowering helps beat heat.

varieties.name: Painted Lady
varieties.growing_notes: Rose-pink bicolor, early flowering. One of best choices for marginal climates. Still requires hill station elevation in Kerala.

varieties.name: Matucana
varieties.growing_notes: Intensely fragrant, magenta-purple. Worth attempting in cool hill stations only.

varieties.name: Early Flowering Types (Mammoth Series)
varieties.growing_notes: Best modern option for Kerala hill stations. Plant November-December for January-March flowering before heat intensifies.
